MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Aug-13-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE PEP BOYS - MANNY MOE & JACK OF CALIFORNIA''S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES

Appearances: Kimberly Chu of Brayton Purcell (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and opposing party; Daniel Hoye of Dentons US LLP (415.267.4000) for defendant and moving party.

Argued and the Court modifies its ruling as follows: denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate the plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent was exposed to products purchased at a Pep Boys store. Defendant failed to negate an essential element of plaintiffs'' claims because even if the declaration of Mr. Cirelli is admissible in its entirety, when read in conjunction with Mr. Paxton''s deposition testimony, it merely creates a triable issue whether decedent purchased auto parts from a Pep Boys store. Plaintiffs'' objections to Joseph Cirelli''s declaration are sustained. Order signed. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Angela Sinclair, CSR 13902 of Aiken Welch (877.451.1580) (503/TLJ)
